ddls:
CREATE TABLE "employees" (
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee */,
  "name" TEXT /* Full name of the employee */,
  "username" TEXT /* Username for system access */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Job title of the employee */,
  "company" TEXT /* Company division the employee belongs to */,
  "websites" TEXT /* List of websites associated with the employee's role */,
  "ssn" TEXT /* Social security number of the employee */,
  "address" TEXT /* Mailing address of the employee */,
  "residence" TEXT /* Residence address of the employee */,
  "blood_group" TEXT /* Blood group of the employee */,
  "sex" TEXT /* Sex of the employee */,
  "mail" TEXT /* Email address of the employee */,
  "birthdate" TEXT /* Birthdate of the employee */,
  "current_location" TEXT /* Current geographical location of the employee */,
  "phone_number" TEXT /* Contact phone number for the employee */,
  "emergency_contact" TEXT /* Emergency contact information */,
  "hire_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was hired */,
  "termination_date" TEXT /* Date when the employee was terminated (if applicable) */,
  "status" TEXT /* Current employment status (e.g., active, inactive, on leave) */,
  "department_id" INTEGER /* Foreign key to the departments table */,
  "manager_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee's manager */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the employee's current location */,
  "preferred_language" TEXT /* Preferred language for communication */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_company FOREIGN KEY ("company") REFERENCES companies ("company_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_current_location FOREIGN KEY ("current_location") REFERENCES locations ("location_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employees_job_title FOREIGN KEY ("job_title") REFERENCES jobs ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "companies" (
  "company_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each company */,
  "company_name" TEXT /* Name of the company */,
  "division" TEXT /* Division the company belongs to */,
  "headquarters" TEXT /* Headquarters location of the company */,
  "ceo" TEXT /* Name of the CEO */,
  "founded_date" TEXT /* Date when the company was founded */,
  "industry" TEXT /* Industry the company operates in */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("company_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "jobs" (
  "job_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each job title */,
  "job_title" TEXT /* Title of the job */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the job belongs to */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the job responsibilities */,
  "salary_range" TEXT /* Salary range for the job title */,
  "required_qualifications" TEXT /* Qualifications required for the job */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("job_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "locations" (
  "location_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each location */,
  "address" TEXT /* Street address of the location */,
  "city" TEXT /* City where the location is situated */,
  "state" TEXT /* State where the location is situated */,
  "country" TEXT /* Country where the location is situated */,
  "postal_code" TEXT /* Postal code of the location */,
  "office_phone" TEXT /* Main office phone number */,
  "fax" TEXT /* Fax number */,
  "timezone" TEXT /* Timezone of the location */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("location_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "user_roles" (
  "role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each role */,
  "role_name" TEXT /* Name of the role */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the role permissions */,
  "level" INTEGER /* Level of the role for hierarchical permissions */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("role_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_roles" (
  "employee_role_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each employee-role mapping */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee */,
  "role_id" INTEGER /* ID of the role assigned to the employee */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("employee_role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_roles_role_id FOREIGN KEY ("role_id") REFERENCES user_roles ("role_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_roles_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "access_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each access log entry */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose record was accessed */,
  "accessed_by_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who accessed the record */,
  "access_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the record was accessed */,
  "access_type" TEXT /* Type of access (e.g., view, edit) */,
  "ip_address" TEXT /* IP address from which the access was made */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_accessed_by_id FOREIGN KEY ("accessed_by_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_access_logs_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "employee_changes" (
  "change_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each change record */,
  "employee_id" INTEGER /* ID of the employee whose record was changed */,
  "changed_by_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the change */,
  "change_date" TEXT /* Date and time when the change was made */,
  "change_type" TEXT /* Type of change (e.g., address update, role change) */,
  "details" TEXT /* Details of the change */,
  "previous_value" TEXT /* Previous value before the change */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("change_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_changes_changed_by_id FOREIGN KEY ("changed_by_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_employee_changes_employee_id FOREIGN KEY ("employee_id") REFERENCES employees ("employee_id")
)
